name: Alcanices - tour around Sejas de Aliste and Rabano de Aliste
name_original: Alcañices – vuelta por Sejas de Aliste y Rábano de Aliste
region_intro: Road cycling around Alcañices offers diverse terrain, characterized by an undulating geography that transitions from dry landscapes to slight mounds. The region is part of the UNESCO Meseta Ibérica transboundary biosphere reserve, featuring well-preserved natural habitats. Cyclists can expect mostly paved surfaces and moderate terrain, with routes traversing areas near the River Angueira and through pine forests in the Sierra de Brunosiños and Sierra de Bozas. Elevated points like Alto de la Atalaya and Teso las Viñas provide scenic vistas. Best road cycling routes around Alcañices The most popular road cycling route is Alcanices - tour around Sejas de Aliste and Rabano de Aliste, a 24.1 miles (38.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the rural landscapes surrounding Alcañices. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alcañices – tour around Mahide and Ferreruela, a difficult 55.3 miles (89.0 km) path. This longer route features significant elevation gain, passing through villages and varied countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Río Manzanas por Sejas de Aliste — circular desde Rábano de Aliste, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through the scenic area around the Río Manzanas, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Road cycling around Alcañices is defined by undulating hills, dry landscapes, and riparian forests along the River Angueira. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Alcañices are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcañices's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Alcañices?
A: Road cycling around Alcañices features an undulating geography, transitioning from dry landscapes to slight mounds. You'll find mostly paved surfaces and moderate terrain, with routes often traversing areas near the River Angueira and through pine forests in the Sierra de Brunosiños and Sierra de Bozas. Elevated points like Alto de la Atalaya and Teso las Viñas offer scenic vistas.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcañices?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Alcañices documented on komoot. These routes offer a mix of moderate and difficult options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes in the Alcañices area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Alcañices offers several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025813577/alcanices-tour-around-mahide-and-ferreruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañices – tour around Mahide and Ferreruela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 55.3-mile (89.0 km) path featuring significant elevation gain as it passes through villages and varied countryside.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is part of the UNESCO Meseta Ibérica transboundary biosphere reserve, offering well-preserved natural habitats. You can expect to cycle alongside the River Angueira with its riparian forests, and through pine forests in the Sierra de Brunosiños and Sierra de Bozas. Scenic elevated points like Alto de la Atalaya and Teso las Viñas provide beautiful views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. Alcañices is rich in history. You can find remains of its ancient castle wall and four towers, the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción, and the Palace of the Marquises of Alcañices. Nearby, you can also appreciate the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Traditional Stone Architecture of Rabanales&lt;/a&gt;. These historical sites offer engaging stops along your routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alcañices?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from undulating hills to riparian forests, and the options available for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Alcañices?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1368833949/rio-manzanas-por-sejas-de-aliste-circular-desde-rabano-de-aliste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Manzanas por Sejas de Aliste — circular desde Rábano de Aliste&lt;/a&gt; is a 19.0-mile (30.6 km) trail that leads through the scenic area around the Río Manzanas, typically completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcañices?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not detailed, the region's climate, typical of inland Spain, suggests spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always advisable.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the network offers options for different ability levels. A moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024727175/alcanices-tour-around-sejas-de-aliste-and-rabano-de-aliste" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcanices - tour around Sejas de Aliste and Rabano de Aliste&lt;/a&gt;, at 24.1 miles (38.7 km), explores rural landscapes and is a good option for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Where can I find parking when cycling in Alcañices?
A: While specific parking locations for cyclists are not detailed, Alcañices is a municipality with a town center. It is generally possible to find parking in or near the town center, especially if you are starting a route directly from there.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with visiting natural attractions?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass by or near notable natural landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt;, known for its sulfur waters, is located in Grisuela, approximately 13 km from Alcañices, making it a potential stop on a longer ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña el Pingón Cave and Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is another prominent natural landmark in a scenic setting.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the border with Portugal?
A: Alcañices is located near the Portuguese border, and its history is tied to the Treaty of Alcañices which defined part of this border. While not explicitly named as 'border routes', the region's proximity means many routes will offer a unique character blending Spanish and Portuguese influences. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369595194/alcanices-smugglers-route-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcañices Smugglers' Route Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that hints at the historical cross-border activity.
tips: Alcañices is a small town on the N-122 national road. The most notable monument is the Clock Tower, one of the four remaining sections of the old wall that surrounded the town. The other interesting monument is the 16th century Franciscan monastery.
A small town on the upper bank river. It has a very low bridge. Nice place to stop and rest in the shade of the trees.
